commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=143a3b015527b63e3110728a3cbf6b92be9b7417)
commands.review_summary:
The branch records AWS SSH CIDRs on lease records and uses active AWS lease CIDRs when creating or refreshing shared security-group ingress, with focused tests and docs updates.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sends only the current request CIDR to AWS ingress refresh, and the AWS provider prunes managed SSH rules outside that desired set. I did not run live AWS reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer review of the upgrade-safe AWS ingress path before merge; ClawSweeper repair should not run while proof is missing.

target.title: fix(worker): preserve active AWS SSH ingress
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes AWS SSH ingress churn by moving active SSH source reconciliation behind provider hooks instead of keeping AWS-specific ingress policy in the fleet core.

The bug: AWS managed leases share the `crabbox-runners` security group. The AWS client treats the CIDR list passed to security-group refresh as the desired managed SSH ingress set and prunes Crabbox-owned rules outside that set. A later AWS create or heartbeat from a different source IP could therefore remove another active lease's SSH ingress.

The clean architecture now:

- Fleet core passes provider-neutral access context: request source CIDRs and active lease/access records.
- AWS provider code owns AWS SSH source state, active CIDR unioning, and additive-vs-authoritative security-group reconciliation.
- In-flight AWS creates publish invisible `provider-access:*` reservations so concurrent creates see pending CIDRs and cost limits still count provisioning leases.
- Global `CRABBOX_AWS_SSH_CIDRS` is used for reconciliation but is not persisted as lease-owned CIDR state.
- Rollout-era active leases without source state force additive reconciliation until their source state is learned or they expire, so unknown existing rules are not pruned.

Docs also add the provider-boundary rule to `AGENTS.md` and `docs/vision.md`.

## Verification

- `npm test --prefix worker -- fleet.test.ts aws.test.ts`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `npm run docs:check`
- `npm run check`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
